description_long: This object is a vintage Nokia communicator phone, featuring a robust black plastic exterior with a prominent antenna on one end. The front side displays a small monochrome screen above a physical keypad with tactile buttons, and the Nokia logo is visible above the display. When opened, the device reveals a full QWERTY keyboard and a larger internal display, designed for messaging and productivity tasks. The back side is plain except for a metallic label and a battery compartment. The phone's design is chunky and utilitarian, with rounded edges and a matte finish, typical of late 1990s mobile devices. Its construction suggests durability, with most of the body made from hard plastic and some internal metal components for structural support.
description: A vintage Nokia communicator phone with a black plastic body, external keypad, antenna, and a fold-out QWERTY keyboard with a larger internal display.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the plain black back, metallic label, and battery compartment.
description_view_1: Front view with the Nokia logo, small display, and physical keypad with buttons.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the thickness, antenna, and the edge of the fold-out keyboard.
description_view_3: Open view displaying the full QWERTY keyboard and large internal screen.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
